daily_summary: Today's top-scoring papers offer significant advancements in AI research, particularly in the areas of benchmarking, reasoning, and multimodal processing. Notably, papers such as "Interactive Benchmarks" and "\$OneMillion-Bench: How Far are Language Agents from Human Experts?" push the boundaries of evaluating AI performance, while "nabla-Reasoner: LLM Reasoning via Test-Time Gradient Descent in Latent Space" and "τ-Knowledge: Evaluating Conversational Agents over Unstructured Knowledge" delve into the intricacies of language model reasoning and knowledge evaluation. Furthermore, innovative approaches like "Penguin-VL: Exploring the Efficiency Limits of VLM with LLM-based Vision Encoders" and "PixARMesh: Autoregressive Mesh-Native Single-View Scene Reconstruction" demonstrate promising directions in vision-language modeling and 3D scene reconstruction.
